what_it_does: Records the completion criteria and live verification status for the Universal Projection build, organized as a truth-table of verifiable FUNCTION/FORM statements per group, with dated evidence, status markers, and explicit gaps.
observations: Verification rules demand FUNCTION claims be proven by live use (curl/browser/MCP), never by reading code or DOM queries alone.
FORM claims must pass a separate design-critic (not the implementer) and machine-gated design-lint against the design-system tokens.
Status taxonomy uses emojis: ✅ verified-by-use, 🟡 designed/stubbed, 🔴 broken/absent.
The 'instrument authors' floor was corrected 2026-06-15: the instrument is not read-only; writes are explicit verbs, with safety via git-revert, not consent-gating.
All twelve build groups (1–12) are marked ✅ complete as of 2026-06-14/15, with acceptance-suite counts and live viewport verification.
A 2026-06-17 legibility gap is flagged as the root failure: Tim cannot read what the surface shows, so he cannot drive it.
The file contains a dense chronological journal of seam work (space-as-variable, first-slice face, DOM mount, brain hooks, annotation write-back, constellation render, tailscale access).
External references (commit hashes, channel IDs, thread IDs, tailnet domain) appear throughout without local definitions.
Model calls previously blocking the build are declared dissolved after Tim confirmed the logic was correct.
standouts: THE BAR is defined as four gates: verified live, all real data, Tim can drive it, and it is interactive.
The 2026-06-17 legibility crisis is identified as the root gap: the surface explains mechanism (angle/radius) but not meaning (what a dot/sector is).
The instrument is explicitly not read-only; 'everything is a variable' means authoring is allowed, with no surprise writes and no autonomous build-dispatch.
All twelve groups are claimed complete, with G10/G9 re-verified after bridge restart and the first-slice face function-complete in-host.
Binary quantization (#1) was built and verified live as a representation axis alongside layer and resolution.
conventions: Dated, signed entries with commit hashes and channel/thread references.
Honest gap notation: 🔴 open, 🟡 partial, with 'SKIP-not-stall' and 'no churn' stances.
Registry-is-truth: no hardcoded sectors, bindings, or poles.
Verbatim Tim quotes used to ratify or correct decisions.
References to acceptance suites by name and check count.
Use of 'green-paint' as a term for fake progress.
concerns: The document is extremely long and mixes criteria, narrative build log, and seam details, making it hard to use as a quick checklist.
Many 🔴 gaps and 🟡 polish items are scattered across sections rather than consolidated.
Heavy reliance on external identifiers (commit hashes, channel IDs, thread IDs) without local definitions.
Some sections repeat/overlap (BUILD STATUS, PROJECTION LANE, GROUP summaries, RE-VERIFICATION).
The 'no code symbols' note means the file is not machine-linkable to code; criteria are manually mapped to groups.
notes: A living, evidence-heavy completion-criteria journal that declares all twelve Universal Projection groups complete but flags a fresh legibility crisis as the current root blocker.
questions: What are the exact contents of RESEARCH-SYNTHESIS.md and IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE.md referenced at the top?
Who or what are DNA, fork, wildcard, recollection, lead, and composition in this context?
What is the SEED equation and where is it documented?
What is the cron that names relation_types and cascade precedes?
What are the precise acceptance-suite commands and where do they live?
What is the company MCP door and how is it configured?
What is the operator-surface-loop.md that now supersedes part of this file?
What is the v9 verdict that gates the full app-face scale-up?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a verifiable truth-table of completion criteria; actual content is a dense, dated build journal that embeds criteria, decisions, verification evidence, and gap notes in narrative form.
apparent_themes: Verification by live use, not code inspection.
Honest status: no green-paint, fail-loud, mark gaps explicitly.
No churn / stand down when blocked.
Instrument authors data; no autonomous self-modifying builds.
Legibility and human meaning must come from the data, not the instrument.
intention_signals: It is trying to enforce a disciplined, evidence-based build loop where every completion claim is tied to live verification, every gap is honestly declared, and design/authority decisions are traceable to named people and fabric threads.
novelty: The explicit 'verification-by-use' rule, the separate-design-critic requirement, the 'no green-paint / no churn' stance, and the correction that the instrument authors rather than is read-only are unusual for a completion-criteria document.
contribution: The verification framework (FUNCTION-by-use, FORM-by-critic, status taxonomy, THE BAR criteria) and the honest gap/stand-down notation provide a reusable discipline for tracking complex multi-agent build completion.
summary_for_embedding: A living completion-criteria document for the Universal Projection instrument that records per-group FUNCTION and FORM verification status, dated evidence, build decisions, and honest gaps.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a verifiable, non-churning truth-table that tracks whether each build group meets live-use, real-data, drivability, and design-quality bars.
suggested_fields: verification_evidence (links to test runs, screenshots, MCP calls)
proposed_edge_kinds: blocked_by (gap blocked by input or external condition)
